title: orthaea oedipus
intro: Orthaea oedipus, also known as the "King's Cup," is a species of orchid native to South America, characterized by its unique, cup-shaped flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Orthaea oedipus include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Orthaea oedipus often involve infusions or decoctions, which can be used to create teas or tinctures that are consumed orally to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
